name: Hiendelaencina from Atienza — circular by the rural path Cañamares – Pradena
name_original: Hiendelaencina desde Atienza — circular por el Camino rural Cañamares – Pradena
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Atienza traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills, rural paths, and areas of natural interest. The region features varied terrain, including routes near reservoirs and through agricultural zones. Elevations can range significantly, with some routes involving notable climbs. The area offers a mix of open country and more secluded sections, suitable for touring cyclists seeking diverse environments. Best touring cycling routes around Atienza The most popular touring cycling route is Alto Rey from Hiendelaencina — circular by the Embalse de Alcorlo, a 39.4 miles (63.5 km) trail that takes 4 hours 21 minutes to complete. This route offers extensive views and challenging ascents around the Embalse de Alcorlo. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Hiendelaencina from Atienza — circular by the rural path Cañamares – Pradena, a difficult 34.1 miles (55.0 km) path. This route explores rural landscapes and traditional paths between villages. Local touring cyclists also love the Imón y Riba de Santiuste desde Atienza — circular por las Salinas de Alto Alcolea, a 28.8 miles (46.4 km) trail leading through salt flats and rural areas, often completed in about 2 hours 54 minutes. Touring cycling around Atienza is defined by rural paths, reservoir views, and varied elevations. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult routes. The routes in Atienza are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 6 reviews. More than 80 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Atienza's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Atienza?
A: There are approximately 15 touring cycling routes around Atienza, offering a variety of experiences across the region's diverse terrain.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly touring cycling routes in Atienza?
A: While there are no routes specifically classified as 'easy', Atienza offers 5 moderate touring cycling routes. These routes provide a good option for those seeking a less strenuous ride compared to the more difficult trails. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1187165475/imon-y-riba-de-santiuste-desde-atienza-circular-por-las-salinas-de-alto-alcolea" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Imón y Riba de Santiuste desde Atienza — circular por las Salinas de Alto Alcolea&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through salt flats and rural areas.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on touring cycling routes around Atienza?
A: The routes around Atienza traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills, rural paths, and areas of natural interest. You'll find varied terrain, including sections near reservoirs, through agricultural zones, and open country, often with significant elevation changes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1185749905/alto-rey-from-hiendelaencina-circular-by-the-embalse-de-alcorlo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto Rey from Hiendelaencina — circular by the Embalse de Alcorlo&lt;/a&gt; offers extensive views around the Alcorlo Reservoir.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Atienza are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1188017037/hiendelaencina-from-atienza-circular-by-the-rural-path-canamares-pradena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiendelaencina from Atienza — circular by the rural path Cañamares – Pradena&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1187248943/barcones-from-atienza-circular-through-the-saladares-of-the-cuenca-del-rio-salado" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barcones from Atienza — circular through the Saladares of the Cuenca del Río Salado&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see along the cycling routes?
A: The Atienza area is rich in historical and natural sights. Along your rides, you might encounter impressive reservoirs like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5388806" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañamares River Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5443712" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pálmaces Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2054767" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Atienza&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent feature, and you can even find a dedicated &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4436053" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Atienza Castle&lt;/a&gt; highlight. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1187211762/el-destierro-camino-del-cid-loop-from-atienza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Destierro - Camino del Cid Loop from Atienza&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, touches upon historical paths.

Q: What is the typical length and duration of touring cycling routes in Atienza?
A: The touring cycling routes around Atienza vary significantly in length and duration. They can range from around 28 miles (46 km) taking about 2 hours 54 minutes, to longer routes of nearly 40 miles (63 km) which might take over 4 hours to complete. This variety allows cyclists to choose routes that fit their available time and fitness levels.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Atienza?
A: The touring cycling routes in Atienza are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.8 out of 5 stars from over 6 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse rural landscapes, the challenging ascents, and the unique features like the salt flats and reservoir views.

Q: Is Atienza a good destination for touring cyclists looking for a challenge?
A: Yes, Atienza is an excellent destination for touring cyclists seeking a challenge. A significant portion of the routes, specifically 10 out of 15, are classified as 'difficult' due to varied terrain and notable elevation changes. These routes offer rewarding ascents and descents across the region's rolling hills.

Q: Are there opportunities to see wildlife while cycling in Atienza?
A: The rural and natural landscapes around Atienza, including areas near reservoirs and agricultural zones, provide opportunities to observe local wildlife. While specific sightings cannot be guaranteed, the secluded sections of many routes increase the chances of encountering various species native to the region.

Q: What is the best season for touring cycling in Atienza?
A: Given the region's characteristics of rolling hills and open country, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for touring cycling in Atienza. Temperatures are milder, and the natural scenery is often at its most vibrant. Summer can be warm, and winter may present colder conditions, especially at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by cafes or places to stop for refreshments?
A: While specific cafes are not detailed for every route, many routes connect villages and rural areas. Planning your route to pass through towns like Atienza, Hiendelaencina, or Imón will likely provide opportunities for refreshments and breaks. It's always advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially on longer or more remote sections.
tips: Hiendelaencina is a historic mining town on the slopes of the Sierra Norte de Guadalajara Natural Park.

One of the starting points of the Alto Rey mountain trail, the small rural town of Prádena de Atienza is a unique place to visit. I love the small local stone houses, typical of the mountain villages in this area.

The Castillo de Atienza, like so many fortifications in the border area between the Moors and the Spaniards, was in rather unstable ownership. From the 9th to the 11th century it was part of the Moorish defense lines south of the Duero and was considered an almost impregnable fortress.
